report_name: CMD Ransomware Hits Mount Royal University, Claims 10TB of Data and Demands $1.9M

threat_actors.name: CMD Organization
threat_actors.description: A relatively new ransomware group that claimed responsibility for the attack on Mount Royal University. The group uses a double-extortion model, exfiltrating data before encrypting or deleting systems, and operates a Tor-based leak site.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion via ransomware and data leakage, with a specific demand of $1.9 million from Mount Royal University.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: Attackers deleted two major file storage systems at Mount Royal University, one containing sensitive data and another for general use, to increase pressure on the victim.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The CMD Organization stole over 10 terabytes of data from Mount Royal University before executing the destructive payload. This suggests prolonged access and the use of tools for large-scale data transfer.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The CMD Organization employed a double-extortion model which involves encrypting systems after data exfiltration to pressure victims into paying a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Cited as a common and possible initial access vector for ransomware attacks on universities, potentially used to harvest user credentials to gain entry into Mount Royal University's network.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Cited as a possible initial access vector, involving the exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities in systems like VPNs or web applications.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely gained and used administrative privileges to access and delete the university's critical file storage systems.

malware.name: CMD Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware used by the CMD Organization to attack Mount Royal University. It performs data destruction and/or encryption as part of a double-extortion scheme.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate data synchronization tool that attackers often use to exfiltrate large volumes of data.

tools.name: MegaSync
tools.description: Mentioned as a legitimate data synchronization tool that attackers often use to exfiltrate data.

tools.name: Net
tools.description: The 'net use' command was mentioned as a tool used by attackers to map network drives for data access and encryption.

identities.name: Mount Royal University
identities.description: A university in Alberta, Canada, that was the victim of a ransomware attack by the CMD Organization, resulting in the theft of over 10TB of student and employee data and the deletion of critical systems.

infrastructure.name: CMD Organization Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A Tor-based leak site used by the CMD Organization to post victim information, proof of breach, and ransom demands as part of their double-extortion scheme.

indicators.name: Suspicious Network Share Mapping
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Network Share Mapping
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net use' to map network shares, a common technique used by ransomware for lateral movement and data access.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/mount-royal-university-confirms-data-stolen-in-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1021.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' use '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'net use' command to map network shares, a common TTP for ransomware to access and encrypt data across a network.

indicators.name: Potential Data Exfiltration Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Exfiltration Tool Execution
id: a3b4c5d6-e7f8-4a9b-8c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools like rclone.exe or megasync.exe, which are often abused by attackers for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/mount-royal-university-confirms-data-stolen-in-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rclone.exe'
      - '\megasync.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of legitimate data synchronization tools (rclone, megasync) that are commonly abused by threat actors for mass data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Suspicious Mass File Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Mass File Deletion
id: b4c5d6e7-f8a9-4b0c-8d1e-2f3a4b5c6d7e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the 'del' command with switches for silent and recursive deletion, which can be indicative of data destruction activities.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/mount-royal-university-confirms-data-stolen-in-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'del '
      - '/s'
      - '/q'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'del /s /q' command line, which can indicate an attempt at mass data destruction as seen in some ransomware attacks.

relationships.target_name: CMD Organization Dark Web Leak Site
